verses.dev: तप्यमाने तपो देवे देवा: सर्षिगणा: पुरा।सेनापतिमभीप्सन्त: पितामहमुपागमन्
verses.trans: In olden days when Iswara was engaged in austerities, the devatas together with the ascetics, approached Lord Brahma for an army general.

verses.dev: ततोऽब्रुवन् सुरास्सर्वे भगवन्तं पितामहम्।प्रणिपत्य सुरास्सर्वे सेन्द्रास्साग्निपुरोगमा:
verses.trans: Then led by Indra and the Firegod all devatas approached Lord Brahma, the grandsire saluted him (with folded hands) and spoke.

verses.dev: यो न स्सेनापतिर्देव दत्तो भगवता पुरा।तप: परममास्थाय तप्यते स्म सहोमया
verses.trans: "O Lord in ancient times your lordship gave us an army general (at the time of creation). He is engrossed in great penance along with Uma .

verses.dev: यदत्रानन्तरं कार्यं लोकानां हितकाम्यया।संविधत्स्व विधानज्ञ त्वं हि न: परमा गति:
verses.trans: "O Knower of law and order O Grandsire verily you are our supreme refuge committed to the welfare of the worlds. Tell us what we should do in this matter (now that we cannot procreate a general on account of Uma's curse)".

words.m: knower of the law and order, O Grandsire

verses.dev: देवतानां वचश्श्रुत्वा सर्वलोकपितामह:।सान्त्वयन्मधुरैर्वाक्यैस्त्रिदशानिदमब्रवीत्
verses.trans: Hearing the words of the devatas, Brahma, the Grandsire of all the worlds consoled the devatas with sweet words, saying:

verses.dev: शैल पुत्र्या यदुक्तं तन्न प्रजास्यथ पत्निषु ।तस्या वचनमक्लिष्टं सत्यमेतन्न संशय:
इयमाकाशगा गङ्गा यस्यां पुत्रं हुताशन:।जनयिष्यति देवानां सेनापतिमरिन्दमम्
verses.trans: "Ganga keeps flowing in the celestial regions. Through Ganga the Firegod will beget a son who will be capable of destroying enemies. He will become the army general of the gods".

verses.dev: ज्येष्ठा शैलेन्द्रदुहिता मानयिष्यति तत्सुतम्।उमायास्तद्बहुमतं भविष्यति न संशय:
verses.trans: "Ganga, the eldest daughter of the king of the mountains, will tend that son of Agni. This will be totally acceptable to Uma. No doubt".

verses.dev: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कृतार्था रघुनन्दन।प्रणिपत्य सुरास्सर्वे पितामहमपूजयन्
verses.trans: "O son of the Raghus having heard Brahma's words all the devatas considered themselves successful in their mission. They saluted him with folded hands and offered their worship.

verses.dev: ते गत्वा पर्वतं राम कैलासं धातुमण्डितम्।अग्निं नियोजयामासु: पुत्रार्थं सर्वदेवता:
verses.trans: All the devatas, went to mount Kailasa, adorned with minerals, and urged the Firegod for a son.

verses.dev: देवकार्यमिदं देव संविधत्स्व हुताशन।शैलपुत्र्यां महातेजो गङ्गायां तेज उत्सृज
verses.trans: 'O lustrous Firegod, accomplish this divine work. O god, possessed of mighty energy, release the vital fluid of Iswara in Ganga, daughter of the king of mountains.

verses.dev: देवतानां प्रतिज्ञाय गङ्गामभ्येत्य पावक:।गर्भं धारय वै देवि देवतानामिदं प्रियम्
verses.trans: "The Firegod gave his consent. The devatas approached Ganga and said, 'O goddess bear the energy of Siva (inyour world) since this cause is dear to the gods'.

verses.dev: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा दिव्यं रूपमधारयत्।दृष्ट्वा तन्महिमानं स समन्तादवकीर्यत
verses.trans: "Hearing the words of the Firegod, Ganga assumed a divine form. Beholding her glory, the god of fire pervaded her from all sides.

verses.dev: समन्ततस्तदा देवीमभ्यषिञ्चत पावक:।सर्वस्रोतांसि पूर्णानि गङ्गाया रघुनन्दन
verses.trans: O Rama then, the Firegod discharged the vital fluid of Iswara, preserved in his body, which pervaded all sides of Ganga and all her streams were filled with (the fluid).

words.m: discharged (sprinkled) the vital fluid of Ishwara preserved in his body

verses.dev: तमुवाच ततो गङ्गा सर्वदेवपुरोहितम्।अशक्ता धारणे देव तव तेज स्समुद्धतम्।दह्यमानाग्निना तेन सम्प्रव्यथितचेतना
verses.trans: Ganga addressing the Firegod standing ahead of all the devatas said 'O god of fire, I am incapable of holding your everincreasing splendour. The fiery fluid is burning me. My consciousness is overwhelmed'.

verses.dev: अथाब्रवीदिदं गङ्गां सर्वदेवहुताशन:।इह हैमवती पादे गर्भोऽयं सन्निवेश्यताम्
verses.trans: Thereafter the Firegod who is the partaker of oblations offered to all deities said to Ganga 'Place this embryo on this slope of Mountain Himavan'.

words.m: partaker of oblations offered to all gods

verses.dev: श्रुत्वा त्वग्निवचो गङगा तं गर्भमतिभास्वरम्।उत्ससर्ज महातेज स्स्रोतोभ्यो हि तदानघ
verses.trans: "O glorious, sinless one, (Rama) hearing the words of the Firegod, Ganga then expelled that resplendent embryo from her streams.

verses.dev: यदस्या निर्गतं तस्मात्तप्तजाम्बूनदप्रभम् ।काञ्चनं धरणीं प्राप्तं हिरण्यममलं शुभम्
verses.trans: The embryo that emerged from Ganga reached the earth. It resembled the lustre of molten gold. It furned into gold auspicious, bright and pure.

words.m: resembling the lustre of the molten gold

verses.dev: ताम्रं कार्ष्णायसं चैव तैक्ष्ण्यादेवाभ्यजायतमलं तस्याभवत्तत्र त्रपु सीसकमेव च।तदेतद्धरणीं प्राप्य नानाधातुरवर्धत
verses.trans: There copper and iron were produced from its acidity, zinc and lead from its residue. Various minerals were formed when that embryo reached the earth.

verses.dev: ताम्रं कार्ष्णायसं चैव तैक्ष्ण्यादेवाभ्यजायतमलं तस्याभवत्तत्र त्रपु सीसकमेव च।तदेतद्धरणीं प्राप्य नानाधातुरवर्धत
verses.trans: There copper and iron were produced from its acidity, zinc and lead from its residue. Various minerals were formed when that embryo reached the earth.

verses.dev: निक्षिप्तमात्रे गर्भे तु तेजोभिरभिरञ्जितम्।सर्वं पर्वतसन्नद्धं सौवर्णमभवद्वनम्
verses.trans: When the embryo was placed (in Ganga) it spread on all sides of the forest on the mountain. Irradiated with lustre the forest appeared golden.

verses.dev: जातरूपमिति ख्यातं तदा प्रभृति राघव।सुवर्णं पुरुषव्याघ्र हुताशनसमप्रभम्तृणवृक्षलतागुल्मं सर्वं भवति काञ्चनम्।
verses.trans: O tiger among men, O scion of the Raghus Then onwards gold which is effulgent like fire, became wellknown as Jatarupa (pure form). The grass, shrubs, creepers and trees of that forest looked golden.

words.m: Jatarupa (born with a form), (इति) ख्यातम् wellknown as
with grass, shrubs, creepers and trees

verses.dev: तं कुमारं ततो जातं सेन्द्रास्सह मरुद्गणा:क्षीरसंभावनार्थाय कृत्तिकास्समयोजन्।
verses.trans: Thereafter Kumara was born (out of Ganga's womb). The gods together with Indra arranged six nymphs (stars) to act as nurses to suckle Kumara as their own offspring.

words.m: with a view to suckle him as their own offspring
six nymphs (stars) to act as nurses

verses.dev: ता: क्षीरं जातमात्रस्य कृत्वा समयमुत्तमम्ददु: पुत्रोऽयमस्माकं सर्वासामिति निश्चिता:।
verses.trans: Those krittikas, having decided among themselves and having made an agreement with the gods saying 'This boy shall become a son to all of us' fed the newborn with their milk (breast milk).

verses.dev: ततस्तु देवता स्सर्वा: कार्तिकेय इति ब्रुवन्पुत्रस्त्रैलोक्यविख्यातो भविष्यति न संशय:।4
verses.trans: Thereafter, all the gods said, 'This son shall become renowned in the three worlds as Karttikeya, (son of krittikas). There is no doubt '.

verses.dev: तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा स्कन्नं गर्भपरिस्रवेस्नापयन् परया लक्ष्म्या दीप्यमानं यथानलम्।
verses.trans: Hearing the words of the gods, the krittikas bathed the baby that descended from the womb of Ganga resembling flaming fire and shining with great beauty.

words.m: when the embryo was flowing from womb

verses.dev: स्कन्द इत्यब्रुवन् देवा: स्कन्नं गर्भपरिस्रवात्कार्तिकेयं महाभागं काकुत्स्थ ज्वलनोपमम्।
verses.trans: O scion of the Kakusthas, Karttikeya lustrous like flaming fire, was very fortunate Descended from the womb of the Ganga, he is named Skanda by the gods.

verses.dev: प्रादुर्भूतं तत: क्षीरं कृत्तिकानामनुत्तमम् षण्णां षडाननो भूत्वा जग्राह स्तनजं पय:।
verses.trans: Then excellent milk surged in the breasts of krittikas. Assuming six mouths, he sucked the milk.

verses.dev: गृहीत्वा क्षीरमेकाह्ना सुकुमारवपुस्तदाअजयत्स्वेन वीर्येण दैत्यसेनागणान् विभु:।
verses.trans: Drinking milk just for a day and with a body still tender he could vanquish hosts of demons by his inborn prowess so capable be was

verses.dev: सुरसेनागणपतिं ततस्तमतुलद्युतिम्अभ्यषिञ्चन् सुरगणा स्समेत्याग्निपुरोगमा:।
verses.trans: With the Firegod in the forefront, all the gods assembled and installed Karttikeya, shining on unsurpassed brilliant, as commander of the gods forces.

verses.dev: एष ते राम गङ्गाया विस्तरोऽभिहितो मयाकुमारसम्भवश्चैव धन्य: पुण्यस्तथैव च।
verses.trans: "O Rama this story of Ganga and that of the birth of Kumara, who was fortunate and auspicious, has been related to you in detail.

verses.dev: भक्तश्च य: कार्तिकेये काकुत्स्थ भुवि मानवः।आयुष्मान् पुत्रपौत्रैश्च स्कन्दसालोक्यतां व्रजेत्
verses.trans: O son of the Kakusthas whosoever reveres Kartikeya on this earth with devotion and faith, shall have a long life, sons and grandsons and after death will reach the world of Skanda". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डेण्डे सप्तत्रिंशस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the thirtyseventh sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.

words.m: being in the same world of Skanda
